entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cultivated chickpea used for production worldwide

entities.name: HELICOVERPA ARMIGERA
entities.description: The pod borer insect, a major constraint to chickpea production

entities.name: RESISTANCE TO POD BORER
entities.description: The ability of chickpea or its wild relatives to resist attack by Helicoverpa armigera

entities.name: WILD RELATIVES OF CHICKPEA
entities.description: Non-cultivated species related to chickpea used for studying resistance

entities.name: GREENHOUSE CONDITIONS
entities.description: Controlled environment where the reaction of wild relatives to H. armigera was studied

entities.name: CICER BIJUGUM
entities.description: A wild relative of chickpea showing high levels of resistance to H. armigera

entities.name: CICER PINNATIFIDUM
entities.description: A wild relative of chickpea showing high levels of resistance and inhibiting pupation

entities.name: CICER CUNEATUM
entities.description: A wild relative of chickpea showing high levels of resistance to H. armigera

entities.name: CICER JUDAICUM
entities.description: A wild relative of chickpea showing high levels of resistance and inhibiting pupation

entities.name: CICER RETICULATUM
entities.description: A wild relative of chickpea showing high levels of resistance to H. armigera

entities.name: CICER ARIETINUM
entities.description: The cultivated chickpea species

entities.name: ANNIGERI
entities.description: A cultivated chickpea variety used as a comparison for pod damage

entities.name: ICCV 2
entities.description: A cultivated chickpea variety used as a comparison for larval weight gain

entities.name: LEAF FEEDING
entities.description: The amount of leaf tissue consumed by H. armigera larvae

entities.name: LARVAL WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of H. armigera larvae, used as an indicator of host suitability

entities.name: HOST SUITABILITY INDEX
entities.description: A measure of how suitable a plant is as a host for the pod borer

entities.name: POD DAMAGE
entities.description: The percentage of pods damaged by H. armigera

entities.name: LARVAL WEIGHT GAIN
entities.description: The percentage increase in weight of larvae during feeding

entities.name: PUPATION
entities.description: The process of larval transformation into a pupa

entities.name: ADULT EMERGENCE
entities.description: The process of the adult insect emerging from the pupal stage

entities.name: ANTIBIOSIS
entities.description: A mechanism of resistance where the host plant adversely affects the biology of the insect

entities.name: RESISTANCE GENES
entities.description: Genes providing resistance to the pod borer, targeted for introgression into cultivated chickpea
